title: RNNs & Time Series Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on Sequence Data, Hidden States, LSTMs, and the Vanishing Gradient.

questions.question: What primary limitation found in standard neural networks and CNNs necessitated the widespread mathematical invention of Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs)?

options.text: Standard networks cannot process raw binary data.
Standard networks crash when encountering empty null datasets.

Standard networks execute entirely independent predictions and lack inherent 'memory' between inputs, rendering them mathematically incapable of natively understanding sequences, time, or sentence context over multiple steps.
Standard networks cannot run on GPUs.

questions.question: How does a standard RNN practically pass historical context down a sequence (e.g., from word 1 to word 2)?

options.text: By writing the previous inputs directly to an AWS S3 bucket.
By establishing a 'Hidden State' (analogous to short-term memory) at step 1, and feeding that exact state directly as supplementary input alongside the new raw data into step 2.
By calculating the cross-entropy loss continuously.
By utilizing a Convolutional image filter.

questions.question: Which of the following datasets would a Recurrent Neural Network theoretically model better than a Convolutional Neural Network?

options.text: A static high-resolution JPEG image array of a cat.
A tabular CSV file featuring the square footage of distinct houses.
An MP3 audio recording of a human speaking a continuous sentence spanning several seconds.
A massive static SQL database of user emails.

questions.question: What famous mathematical phenomenon inherently destroyed the ability of traditional 'Vanilla' RNNs to remember crucial context from the beginning of a long paragraph?

options.text: The Traveling Salesman Problem
The Curse of Dimensionality
The Vanishing Gradient Problem (the calculus multipliers shrunk to zero iterating backwards through the enormous sequence length).
The Out of Memory Exception.

questions.question: How does an LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) network specifically combat the flaws of older RNNs?

options.text: It abandons recurrent loops entirely.

It introduces a complex internal 'Cell' architecture featuring explicit, trainable mathematical 'Gates' (Forget, Input, Output) designed to strictly regulate exactly what information is structurally written to or deleted from long-term memory.
It acts purely as an Unsupervised clustering algorithm.
It switches execution directly to CPU processors.
